table_summary: This table records individual loan transaction entries, including transaction identifiers, account codes, transaction dates, amounts, currencies, and exchange rates for each loan-related movement.

column_descriptions.description: Numeric value serving as the primary journal number for the transaction. Used to group or uniquely identify a set of related transaction items.
Numeric value representing the item number within the journal entry. Uniquely identifies each transaction line within a journal (txn_jrno).
Date value indicating when the transaction occurred. Essential for filtering and chronological analysis of loan transactions.
Text (up to 20 characters) representing the account code associated with the transaction. This is typically a unique identifier for a loan account or customer account involved in the transaction.

Three-character text code indicating the currency of the transaction (e.g., 'MNT' for Mongolian Tugrik, 'USD' for US Dollar, 'EUR' for Euro, etc.). This determines the currency context for the txn_amount column.
Numeric value representing the amount of the transaction. The currency for this amount is specified by the cur_code column (e.g., if cur_code is 'USD', txn_amount is in US Dollars).

Numeric value representing the exchange rate applicable to the transaction. This is typically the rate between the transaction currency (cur_code) and a base currency (often MNT or USD), used for reporting or conversion purposes.
Text (up to 9 characters) representing the transaction type or code. This categorizes the nature of the transaction (e.g., disbursement, repayment, fee) for business or accounting purposes.
